source: Anonymous drug-detail request with a caller-selected itemSeq.
closest_control: The shared WebClient has body-size and connection-pool controls but no end-to-end response deadline.
sink: A blocking Mono.zip waits for permission, narrative, and four DUR branches.
impact: No independent impact beyond the already retained shared timeout-root candidate R01-CAN-072.
preconditions: At least one configured government upstream accepts the request and then stalls after connection establishment.
The public drug-detail route is externally reachable in the deployed ingress.
counterevidence: The six calls run concurrently rather than serially.
Fixture-only or unconfigured clients avoid the live network sink.

evidence.claim: The common public WebClient configures memory and pool behavior but no response/read/whole-request timeout.
Drug detail joins all branches and blocks without a duration.

affected_locations.detail: Mono.zip(...).block() waits for permission, EasyDrug, and DUR assembly without a duration.
affected_locations.label: amplifier

fix_direction: Address once at the shared WebClient/deadline boundary under R01-CAN-072; do not track a second finding.
review_tier: none — duplicate of retained shared-root finding
rationale: Validated behavior is real, including a stalled-header/body probe, but this row has the same source, missing control, sink class, impact, and fix as R01-CAN-072.

source: Any request reaching a drug government client.
closest_control: The shared public WebClient lacks response/read/whole-operation deadlines.
sink: Permission, EasyDrug, and DUR clients call block() on provider responses.
impact: No independent impact beyond R01-CAN-072's shared timeout-root finding.
preconditions: A provider or intermediary keeps a response incomplete after connection.
A live or hybrid network path is selected.
counterevidence: Reactor Netty still supplies finite connect and pool-acquire defaults.
Fixture mode does not call the provider.

evidence.claim: All drug clients inherit the same timeout-free public WebClient.
Permission lookup terminates in an undelimited block().

fix_direction: Use the single shared deadline/control fix tracked by R01-CAN-072.
review_tier: none — duplicate of retained shared-root finding
rationale: This is the general statement of the same validated root already represented by R01-CAN-072, so a separate report would double count.

source: Anonymous name, ingredient, or itemSeq request.
closest_control: Shared WebClient pool and body cap exist, but there is no response deadline.
sink: DrugPermissionApiClient blocks on an incomplete provider response.
impact: No independent impact beyond R01-CAN-072.
preconditions: The permission provider accepts but does not complete the response.
The deployed route is exposed to a caller who can cause cache misses.
counterevidence: Successful and explicit-error responses terminate normally.
Six-hour caching reduces repeat calls for completed nonempty responses.

evidence.claim: The permission request calls block() without a timeout.
No response/read/overall timeout is configured.

fix_direction: Fix at the shared deadline boundary represented by R01-CAN-072.

rationale: Concrete reachability is confirmed, but it is one call-site instance of the retained shared timeout-root issue.

A cache miss reaches the live or hybrid client.

The inherited client has no response deadline.

source: Anonymous drug-detail request that misses the DUR caches.
closest_control: Four-way parallelism bounds concurrency, but the inherited client has no response deadline.
sink: warningsFor waits for four blocking DUR operations.
impact: No independent impact beyond R01-CAN-072.
preconditions: One DUR operation accepts and then stalls.
A cold product/kind cache key reaches the provider.
counterevidence: Only four warning kinds are launched per product.
Fixture-only mode and cached completed results avoid the network.

evidence.claim: Four warning kinds are launched and each provider read blocks without a deadline.
No shared response/read/overall deadline exists.

source: Unauthenticated GET /api/v1/facilities with type=pharmacy, open_now=true, and attacker-selected coordinates.
closest_control: Candidate and concurrency caps limit one request to 100 schedule lookups at concurrency four; six-hour caches only help repeated keys.
sink: One cold list lookup plus up to 100 cold weekly-hours calls spend the server's pharmacy credential.
impact: Roughly ten cold populated requests can consume the documented 1,000-call daily budget and deny pharmacy availability to all users.
preconditions: The route is exposed without an upstream-aware caller budget.
Queries select distinct rounded cells and return enough distinct HPIDs to miss both caches.
counterevidence: Per-request work is capped at 101 calls and schedule concurrency is four.
Fixture mode avoids live quota consumption; populated results and cold keys are required.

evidence.claim: The public route exposes coordinates and open_now without admission control.
open_now selects up to 100 candidates and resolves every schedule before limiting the response.
The source documents a 1,000-call daily budget and issues list plus per-HPID calls.

affected_locations.detail: Every pharmacy map request begins with findNear.

affected_locations.detail: Each new approximately 100-metre coordinate grid makes one scarce-quota request and block() has no timeout.
affected_locations.label: root_control

affected_locations.detail: The endpoint is public and allows open_now=true with a 10 km radius.
affected_locations.label: public_entrypoint

affected_locations.detail: The service documents 100 open-now candidates and the 1,000-call pharmacy daily budget.

affected_locations.detail: Each cold HPID causes a separate weekly-hours upstream request.

affected_locations.detail: Six-hour caching reduces repeats but does not impose a caller or daily call budget.

fix_direction: Add an upstream-budget-aware anonymous rate/concurrency gate and cache/coalesce schedule work while preserving honest upstream-failure reporting.
review_tier: P1 — core care-information availability
rationale: The target fixes MAX_OPEN_NOW_CANDIDATES at 100 and resolves every selected candidate's weekly hours before applying the returned-result limit, so a cold populated request can spend up to 101 provider calls.

source: Pharmacy facility request.
closest_control: Response limit and open_now candidate caps constrain schedule enrichment.
sink: FacilityService calls weeklyHours for selected candidates.
impact: The only security-relevant fan-out is already fully represented by R01-CAN-082.
preconditions: open_now=true and a populated cold list select many candidates.
HPID schedule keys are uncached.
counterevidence: When open_now=false, candidate selection is bounded by the requested result limit.
open_now=true is capped at 100 and concurrency four.

evidence.claim: The service selects bounded candidates and enriches them with weekly hours.
Each cold selected HPID maps to one schedule request.

fix_direction: Track fan-out and quota admission under R01-CAN-082 only.
review_tier: none — duplicate of retained quota-amplification finding
rationale: The title overstates ordinary searches; only open_now expands to 100, and that exact source/control/sink is retained as R01-CAN-082.

source: Unauthenticated hospital facility search with attacker-selected exact coordinates, radius, and open_now=true.
closest_control: MAX_PAGES=20, MAX_OPEN_NOW_CANDIDATES=100, and detail concurrency four bound one request but no caller/upstream budget exists.
sink: A cold request can issue 20 sequential HIRA list calls followed by 100 detail calls.
impact: Repeated exact-coordinate misses can exhaust provider capacity and deny hospital/open-now information during care decisions.
preconditions: The public route is externally reachable and HIRA returns enough totalCount/results.
The attacker varies exact coordinate/radius keys to avoid the six-hour cache.
counterevidence: One request is capped at 120 calls and detail concurrency is four.
Fixture mode avoids live HIRA calls; provider-side quota characteristics are not established.

open_now resolves up to 100 hospital details before response limiting.

fix_direction: Introduce endpoint admission and an aggregate upstream-call budget, and make the cache key/cardinality policy resistant to coordinate churn.
review_tier: P1 — core care-information availability
rationale: The target caps list paging at 20 and open-now detail candidates at 100; those bounds limit one request but still leave strong anonymous amplification across unique exact-coordinate keys.

source: Unauthenticated GET /api/v1/geocode with a nonblank address up to 200 characters.
closest_control: Input length and a five-result output cap exist; no endpoint rate, cache, or request coalescing control exists.
sink: Every accepted query sends a new credentialed Naver geocoding request.
impact: Automated unique queries can consume the application's provider quota or billable capacity and degrade map search.
preconditions: The geocode route is internet reachable in production.
Naver meters, charges, or throttles the configured credential at a practically relevant level.
counterevidence: Each request produces exactly one upstream call and returns at most five results.
The provider quota/billing contract and production ingress controls were not available in target evidence.
proof_gaps: Confirm Naver plan quotas, billing, and production edge-rate controls before final prioritization.

evidence.claim: The anonymous route accepts arbitrary bounded address strings and directly invokes the client.
Every accepted configured query makes a fresh request with server credentials.
The backend holds the Naver client secret used by the proxy.

affected_locations.detail: The public GET route accepts any nonblank address up to 200 characters without authentication or requester quota.
affected_locations.label: entrypoint

affected_locations.detail: Every accepted query makes a fresh credentialed Naver request; neither this method nor the controller is cached.
affected_locations.label: sink

affected_locations.detail: The backend supplies the server-side client ID and secret for each anonymous request.
affected_locations.label: credential_boundary

affected_locations.detail: The fixed-authority client caps response memory but supplies no rate, quota, cache, or overall timeout control.
affected_locations.label: root_control

affected_locations.detail: GET /api/v1/geocode accepts every nonblank query up to 200 characters and directly calls the server-side Naver client without authentication, caching, or endpoint-local throttling.
affected_locations.label: public_trigger

affected_locations.detail: Every configured search sends a new Naver request carrying server-owned API key headers. MAX_RESULTS=5 is enforced only after the upstream response and does not limit request count.
affected_locations.label: credentialed_upstream_sink

affected_locations.detail: The proxy is specifically configured with the server-only NAVER_MAP_CLIENT_SECRET, so anonymous demand spends the application's upstream identity rather than a caller-provided credential.
affected_locations.label: server_secret_configuration

fix_direction: Add cache/coalescing and caller-aware rate admission sized to the confirmed Naver plan; keep credentials server-side.
review_tier: P2 — conditional credentialed-provider resource abuse
rationale: The missing local control is proven, but external quota, billing, and deployment-edge controls remain provider/infrastructure facts and are not inferred.

source: Anonymous drug search with a caller-selected 2..100-character name or ingredient.
closest_control: Six-hour exact-key caching and a 20-result cap reduce repeats but do not limit distinct keys or caller rate.
sink: Each fresh name/ingredient key issues one credentialed government request and creates a Redis entry.
impact: High-cardinality queries can consume provider quota, servlet capacity, and Redis memory.
preconditions: The route is reachable without an external rate control.
Attackers generate many distinct legal search strings while live or hybrid upstream calls are configured.
counterevidence: Each cold key causes one upstream call, not fan-out.
Six-hour TTL, 4 MiB response cap, and provider validation bound per-key resource use.
proof_gaps: Confirm production ingress throttling and provider quota policy.

evidence.claim: The anonymous route only bounds each selected search value by length.
Exact caller strings become cache keys and each miss performs one provider call.
Public API cache entries live six hours without a repository-level cardinality limit.

affected_locations.detail: Anonymous GET /api/v1/drugs accepts a caller-selected name or ingredient; only each string's 2..100 length is bounded.
affected_locations.label: entrypoint

affected_locations.detail: The exact attacker-selected value is the six-hour cache key, so randomized legal values force distinct misses and entries.
affected_locations.label: root_control

affected_locations.detail: Each miss issues a paid/quota-bearing government API request and blocks the servlet path with no application deadline.
affected_locations.label: sink

affected_locations.detail: The shared public WebClient config sets DNS and a 4 MiB body cap but no connect, response, read, or overall timeout.
affected_locations.label: root_control

affected_locations.detail: Public API cache entries remain in Redis for six hours and no cache-size/cardinality bound is configured.

affected_locations.detail: Each cold key issues a government API request.
affected_locations.label: sink

fix_direction: Normalize/bound cache keys and add caller/upstream-budget admission without weakening result validation.
review_tier: P2 — bounded resource-amplification exposure
rationale: Attacker-expandable keys and the network sink are source-proven; single-call amplification is low, so this is below the care-availability P1 cases.

source: Anonymous GET /api/v1/drugs/{itemSeq} with an unconstrained caller-selected identifier.
closest_control: Parallel assembly reduces latency and caches completed results, but itemSeq has no format/rate admission and empty permission/narrative results are not cached.
sink: One cold identifier starts permission, EasyDrug, and four DUR operations before existence is known.
impact: Distinct or repeated nonexistent identifiers can multiply provider usage and occupy request threads, degrading drug and chat availability.
preconditions: The detail route is exposed without an external admission control.
Live/hybrid clients are configured and attacker identifiers miss relevant caches.
counterevidence: The six operations run concurrently and DUR kind count is fixed at four.
Some completed nonempty responses are cached for six hours.

evidence.claim: The public itemSeq path has no format or application-rate constraint.
Detail assembly starts permission, EasyDrug, and DUR branches together.
DUR expands one detail into four provider operations.

affected_locations.detail: Anonymous GET /api/v1/drugs/{itemSeq} accepts a caller-selected product identifier without authentication or application-level size/rate control.
affected_locations.label: entrypoint

affected_locations.detail: One detail request concurrently joins permission detail, e약은요 detail, and DUR warnings and blocks for all results.
affected_locations.label: root_control

affected_locations.detail: The DUR branch expands into four independently issued upstream operations and each ends in an undelimited block().
affected_locations.label: concrete_implementation

affected_locations.detail: Permission detail adds one more attacker-keyed upstream operation; missing Optional values are deliberately not cached.
affected_locations.label: sink

affected_locations.detail: Consumer-guidance detail adds a sixth operation; missing Optional values are also not cached.
affected_locations.label: sink

affected_locations.detail: The common public client has a body cap but no response/connect/read/overall timeout.

affected_locations.detail: itemSeq has no length/format constraint and the route is anonymous.

affected_locations.detail: One detail request concurrently starts permission, EasyDrug, and four DUR operations.

affected_locations.detail: Optional.empty is intentionally excluded from Redis, so the same nonexistent key always reaches e약은요.
affected_locations.label: root_control

affected_locations.detail: The permission-detail sibling independently excludes an empty Optional from its cache.

fix_direction: Validate itemSeq shape, short-circuit dependent work after identity resolution, negative-cache bounded misses, and add caller/upstream-budget admission.
review_tier: P1 — core drug-information availability
rationale: The target deterministically starts six operations per cold itemSeq and deliberately leaves empty permission/narrative results uncached.

source: Anonymous hospital searches with attacker-selected floating-point latitude, longitude, and radius.
closest_control: Entries expire after six hours, but exact coordinates form the key and repository cache configuration sets no memory/cardinality bound.
sink: Each distinct tuple stores a hospital batch containing up to 2,000 rows in Redis.
impact: Coordinate churn can fill Redis and repeatedly trigger large HIRA fetches, denying cached care-location data.
preconditions: Production uses the Redis cache manager and the route is externally reachable.
No deployment-level Redis maxmemory/eviction or edge admission control compensates for the repository configuration.
counterevidence: Latitude, longitude, and radius are range-bounded, and entries have a six-hour TTL.
List paging is capped at 20 pages and response bodies at 4 MiB.
proof_gaps: Confirm production Redis maxmemory/eviction and ingress rate controls.

evidence.claim: Anonymous callers choose hospital coordinates and radius.
Exact double-coordinate/radius tuples key batches of up to 2,000 hospital rows.
Entries live six hours and no cache-cardinality or memory policy is set here.

affected_locations.detail: Every distinct double-coordinate/radius tuple receives a separate cache entry containing as many as 2,000 hospital records.
affected_locations.label: root_control

affected_locations.detail: Entries live for six hours and the repository cache configuration has no entry-count or memory bound.
affected_locations.label: sink

fix_direction: Quantize or otherwise canonicalize bounded location keys, cap cache weight/cardinality, and add miss admission aligned with HIRA capacity.
review_tier: P2 — bounded cache-resource amplification with deployment proof gaps
rationale: Target code exposes effectively unbounded exact keys for large values; current local Redis observation also used no maxmemory/eviction, while production settings remain a proof gap.

source: Anonymous pharmacy searches with attacker-selected coordinates.
closest_control: Coordinates are rounded to 0.001 degrees and entries expire after six hours, but no aggregate cache weight/cardinality budget exists.
sink: Each distinct rounded cell stores up to 100 pharmacy rows in Redis and may trigger a provider list call.
impact: Sustained cell churn can consume Redis memory and pharmacy quota, degrading facility search.
preconditions: The route is reachable without a stronger edge budget.
Production uses repository-equivalent Redis capacity/eviction settings.
counterevidence: Rounding substantially reduces the keyspace versus exact doubles.
Per-entry rows are capped at 100 and TTL is six hours.
proof_gaps: Confirm production Redis maxmemory/eviction and edge admission controls.

evidence.claim: Public callers control coordinates.
The cache key is a 0.001-degree cell and each miss can store 100 rows.
No aggregate cache-size control is configured in target code.

affected_locations.detail: Attacker coordinates are rounded to a 0.001-degree grid, but every distinct grid retains a batch of up to 100 rows.
affected_locations.label: root_control

fix_direction: Enforce a bounded weighted cache and per-caller/miss admission; retain location rounding and provider-error honesty.
review_tier: P2 — slower bounded-per-entry cache exhaustion
rationale: The rounded grid still has approximately 64.8 billion theoretical cells, but per-entry size, TTL, and the effort to traverse cells keep it below P1.

source: User-selected hospital or pharmacy search coordinates.
closest_control: Six-hour cache TTL and a non-public backing-service design reduce exposure, but raw hospital coordinates remain in key names.
sink: Hospital exact coordinates/radius and pharmacy rounded coordinates are written as Redis keys and can enter persistence/operational tooling.
impact: Redis operators, backups, monitoring, or a cache compromise could reveal sensitive search locations.
preconditions: Search coordinates are treated as personal data under the applicable policy and can be linked to a person or session by surrounding telemetry.
Redis keys or persistence are accessible to an unauthorized or overbroad operational audience.
counterevidence: The keys contain no user identifier and pharmacy coordinates are rounded.
The repository does not expose Redis publicly; entries have a six-hour logical TTL.
proof_gaps: Confirm production Redis persistence/backups, access controls, key telemetry, and the project's classification/retention policy for location searches.
Determine whether any request/session identifiers are correlated with cache operations outside the target repository.

evidence.claim: Hospital keys contain exact lat, lng, and radius.
Pharmacy keys contain rounded coordinates.
The logical cache retention is six hours.

affected_locations.detail: Exact lat,lng,radius key.
affected_locations.label: hospital key

affected_locations.detail: Rounded coordinate key.
affected_locations.label: pharmacy key

affected_locations.detail: Six-hour TTL.
affected_locations.label: retention

fix_direction: Hash or tokenize location keys if policy requires, minimize precision, and align persistence/access/retention with an explicit location-data threat model.
review_tier: P2 — privacy review depends on deployment and policy
rationale: The data flow is proven, but reportability depends on unprovided personal-data classification, correlation, Redis access, and persistence facts.

source: A malformed or compromised pharmacy provider returns more rows than requested.
closest_control: The WebClient body cap and downstream MAX_OPEN_NOW_CANDIDATES=100 bound follow-up work even though parsing accepts all rows.
sink: PharmacyApiClient constructs rows from every returned item.
impact: The candidate's claimed thousands of follow-up schedule calls cannot occur in the target service.
preconditions: The provider violates numOfRows and returns many parseable rows within 4 MiB.
The list call reaches the live provider.
counterevidence: FacilityService caps open-now candidates at 100 before weekly-hours resolution.
The 4 MiB body cap bounds parsing/allocation even under a provider violation.

evidence.claim: The parser maps all body items without a row-count assertion.
Candidate selection caps schedule follow-up at 100 before enrichment.
The shared client limits decoded response bodies to 4 MiB.

fix_direction: Optional defense-in-depth may reject provider row-count contract violations, but no security finding is retained.
review_tier: none — claimed amplification is defeated
rationale: The parser behavior exists, but the downstream cap disproves the candidate's material security impact.

source: Malformed or compromised HIRA hospital rows carrying a negative finite distance.
closest_control: Coordinate finiteness is checked, but distance sign/range is not; downstream code trusts any nonnull provider distance.
sink: A negative distance passes radius filtering, sorts first, and is returned/displayed as the caller's distance.
impact: Far or irrelevant hospitals can displace nearby care locations and mislead users choosing where to seek care.
preconditions: HIRA or an intermediary supplies a negative distance while coordinates remain parseable.
The row reaches the live/hybrid hospital path and is within the returned candidate set.
counterevidence: The normal HIRA provider is expected to return metres and correct nonnegative values.
Fixture rows are controlled repository data.
proof_gaps: Obtain a provider contract/sample establishing the allowed distance domain; the exploit path otherwise requires malformed upstream data.

evidence.claim: Hospital parsing validates coordinate finiteness but not distance sign.
Numeric parsing accepts finite negative values.
Filtering, sorting, and output trust every nonnull provider distance.

affected_locations.detail: Coordinates are checked for finiteness but distance is accepted without a finite or non-negative check.
affected_locations.label: root_control

affected_locations.detail: Every non-null upstream distance is trusted for radius filtering, sorting, and the response card.
affected_locations.label: sink

fix_direction: Validate nonnegative plausible distances at the provider boundary or recompute from validated coordinates before radius/order decisions.
review_tier: P1 — integrity of care-location information
rationale: The impossible-value tell is a negative distance: target logic deterministically treats it as nearer than zero and within every positive radius.

source: Malformed or compromised pharmacy rows with nonfinite/out-of-range coordinates or a negative distance.
closest_control: Only coordinate presence is required; numeric domain validation and nonnegative-distance validation are absent.
sink: Invalid coordinates/distances pass radius/sort and reach facility responses and map-marker construction.
impact: Users can receive impossible locations or wrong nearest-pharmacy ordering, hiding usable care locations or breaking map rendering.
preconditions: The public provider or fixture input contains malformed numeric fields that still parse.
The malformed row reaches the selected candidate set.
counterevidence: The normal government provider is expected to emit valid coordinates and kilometre distances.
The frontend framework renders values as data/text rather than executing markup.
proof_gaps: Confirm the public API's numeric guarantees and observe browser behavior for NaN/infinite coordinates.

evidence.claim: The parser requires nonnull coordinates but does not enforce finite geographic ranges or nonnegative distance.
Generic numeric parsing admits NaN, infinity, and negative values.
Present provider distances and coordinates drive filter/order/output without domain checks.

affected_locations.detail: Only null is rejected; non-finite latitude/longitude and negative or non-finite distance values are accepted.
affected_locations.label: root_control

affected_locations.detail: String values are parsed with Double.parseDouble, which accepts NaN and Infinity as non-null doubles.
affected_locations.label: root_control

affected_locations.detail: A non-null negative distance is trusted for filtering and an accepted row's coordinates are returned to map consumers.
affected_locations.label: sink

fix_direction: Validate coordinate ranges and nonnegative kilometre distance at the provider boundary, failing the affected row closed as unknown/unusable.
review_tier: P1 — integrity and availability of care-location information
rationale: Impossible numeric values are accepted at the trust boundary and become decision/display inputs without a compensating check.

source: Malformed HIRA hospital opening/closing strings.
closest_control: FacilityOperation has an UNKNOWN state, but OpenInterval.of maps malformed time text to the CLOSED sentinel; WeeklyHours therefore preserves no parse-failure signal.
sink: FacilityService reports isOpenNow=false or filters the hospital out for open_now searches.
impact: The app tells a sick user a potentially open hospital is closed, explicitly routing around §2-3's unknown-not-closed invariant.
preconditions: At least one current-day hospital schedule field is malformed while another day is valid enough for hasAnySchedule to be true.
The row is otherwise selected for the caller.
counterevidence: A completely absent schedule remains UNKNOWN because hasAnySchedule is false.
Well-formed provider times evaluate normally.

evidence.claim: Malformed HHMM text produces null and OpenInterval.of converts a null endpoint to the CLOSED sentinel.
Weekly construction does not preserve malformed-versus-absent state.
Hospital operation evaluation consumes the collapsed schedule state and open-now filtering excludes the resulting false status.

affected_locations.detail: Any nonblank start/end strings are inserted without distinguishing valid, closed, and malformed intervals.
affected_locations.label: root_control

affected_locations.detail: Invalid HHMM text and the provider's closed marker both become the same CLOSED sentinel.
affected_locations.label: root_control

affected_locations.detail: If another day is valid, hasAnySchedule succeeds and the malformed current day is emitted as officially CLOSED and excluded by open_now=true.
affected_locations.label: sink

fix_direction: Preserve malformed schedule state as UNKNOWN and ensure open_now filtering never equates an unparseable schedule with closed; this §2 change requires human safety review.
review_tier: P0 — explicit AGENTS §2-3 invariant bypass
rationale: A malformed current-day value is an impossible-value tell that deterministically becomes CLOSED rather than Hours unknown.

source: Malformed pharmacy weekly-hours strings.
closest_control: OpenInterval maps parse failures to CLOSED and the timetable parser/WeeklyHours cannot preserve the failure state.
sink: FacilityService emits isOpenNow=false or removes the pharmacy from open_now results.
impact: A potentially open pharmacy is represented as closed, explicitly violating §2-3.
preconditions: The current-day pharmacy hours are malformed and at least one other interval makes the schedule appear present.
The pharmacy is otherwise in radius and selected.
counterevidence: A fully missing schedule remains UNKNOWN.
Well-formed pharmacy time fields are handled normally.

evidence.claim: Provider duty-time strings are mapped into weekly hours without a malformed-state channel.
Malformed HHMM text produces null and OpenInterval.of converts a null endpoint to CLOSED.
Pharmacy operation evaluation collapses malformed hours and open-now filtering excludes the resulting false status.

affected_locations.detail: Any pair of non-null duty-time strings is accepted, while a one-sided malformed pair is silently omitted.
affected_locations.label: root_control

affected_locations.detail: If some other day is valid, a malformed current-day interval is treated as officially closed and filtered from open_now results.
affected_locations.label: sink

fix_direction: Represent malformed hours as UNKNOWN and propagate that state through open_now filtering and UI copy; require human review because this directly changes §2-3 behavior.
review_tier: P0 — explicit AGENTS §2-3 invariant bypass
rationale: The null-returning HHMM parse branch proves a potentially unknowable schedule is server-authored as closed.

source: Facility search on a Korean public holiday.
closest_control: HolidayCalendar is injected as the closest control but always returns false; holiday-specific upstream fields are not used.
sink: WeeklyHours evaluates the ordinary weekday timetable and emits open/closed status.
impact: Users can be sent to a closed facility or steered away from an open one on holidays.
preconditions: The requested current date is a Korean public holiday.
A facility's holiday hours differ from its ordinary weekday hours.
counterevidence: Non-holiday dates are unaffected.
Some facilities may coincidentally keep weekday hours on a holiday.

evidence.claim: The production holiday predicate is a TODO and unconditionally false.
Both pharmacy and hospital open-state paths rely on that predicate.
Open-state evaluation selects weekday versus holiday behavior from the false flag.

affected_locations.detail: The documented placeholder returns false unconditionally even though the comment acknowledges that the classification is wrong on roughly 15 dates per year.
affected_locations.label: root_control

affected_locations.detail: The false holiday value selects weekday schedules, creates OPEN/CLOSED facility cards, and controls inclusion in open_now=true results.
affected_locations.label: sink

fix_direction: Implement a reviewed Korean-holiday source and preserve UNKNOWN when holiday hours cannot be determined; validate both facility types.
review_tier: P1 — wrong care-location availability information
rationale: The target never enters holiday evaluation in production, so ordinary weekday hours are asserted even when the calendar says holiday.

source: Two callers in the same 0.001-degree cache cell with different exact origins.
closest_control: Rounding bounds cache cardinality, but the cached batch retains provider distance values computed for the first caller and carries no canonical origin correction.
sink: Later radius filtering, ordering, and displayed distance trust the first caller's distances.
impact: A later user can see wrong nearest ordering or have a pharmacy incorrectly included/excluded near a radius boundary.
preconditions: Two searches share a rounded key before the six-hour TTL expires.
Provider distances are origin-dependent and at least one result lies near an ordering/radius boundary.
counterevidence: Origins in one cell are geographically close, limiting absolute error.
Cache expiry bounds duration and exact pharmacy coordinates remain available.
proof_gaps: Quantify maximum error against product radius thresholds and provider distance precision.

evidence.claim: The cache key rounds origin while the cached batch comes from the first exact query.
The service trusts cached nonnull distances for filter, order, and response.
The shared batch can remain for six hours.

affected_locations.detail: Roughly 100 m grid.
affected_locations.label: colliding key

affected_locations.detail: Batch omits request origin.
affected_locations.label: missing origin

affected_locations.detail: Uses first caller distance.
affected_locations.label: trusted stale distance

fix_direction: Cache origin-independent facility facts or recompute distance from validated coordinates for each caller before filtering and ordering.
review_tier: P1 — integrity of care-location ordering/eligibility
rationale: The cache deliberately aliases distinct origins while downstream logic treats the aliased distance as caller-specific.

source: Any pharmacy request served from the six-hour cached list or weekly-hours data.
closest_control: The server owns SourceRef, but cached batches/timetables carry no original retrieval timestamp.
sink: FacilityService stamps Instant.now and presents the cached facts as retrieved at response time.
impact: Users and the model receive false provenance about when availability facts were obtained, directly violating §2-9.
preconditions: A cache hit occurs after the original provider retrieval.
The response includes sourceRef/retrievedAt as designed.
counterevidence: The agency identity remains server-authored and correct.
Cache TTL limits staleness to six hours but does not make response time equal retrieval time.

evidence.claim: PharmacyBatch stores query origin and rows but no retrieval timestamp.
WeeklyHours likewise carries no retrieval provenance.
The service creates a current timestamp and writes it into sourceRef even on cache hits.

affected_locations.detail: Uses request time.
affected_locations.label: current-time stamp

affected_locations.detail: Batch omits timestamp.
affected_locations.label: missing fetch time

affected_locations.detail: No fetch timestamp.
affected_locations.label: missing schedule time

fix_direction: Carry immutable retrieval provenance with cached data and reuse it on responses; this explicit §2-9 change requires human review.
review_tier: P0 — explicit AGENTS §2-9 provenance invariant bypass
rationale: A cache hit is the settling observation: no provider retrieval occurs, yet the response timestamp is freshly authored as though it did.

source: Anonymous pharmacy search in fixture mode or a fixture fallback with arbitrary coordinates.
closest_control: Fixture labeling warns that data are sample/stale, but query parameters are ignored and captured distance values are retained.
sink: FacilityService prefers captured nonnull distances for radius/order and the map describes them relative to the current center.
impact: A remote caller can be told a Seoul fixture pharmacy is only hundreds of metres away, corrupting nearby-care decisions.
preconditions: Fixture or fallback data are selected.
Caller coordinates differ materially from the fixture capture origin.
counterevidence: Fixture data are visibly labeled as sample rather than live.
Exact facility coordinates are present and could support recomputation, but target code does not use them when distance exists.

evidence.claim: Fixture/fallback parsing ignores the caller query and returns the captured batch.
Captured rows include fixed Seoul coordinates and origin-dependent distances.
Captured distance drives radius, order, and output instead of recomputation.

affected_locations.detail: Any anonymous caller selects valid latitude/longitude, radius, and type=pharmacy.
affected_locations.label: anonymous_entrypoint

affected_locations.detail: Fixture-only, missing-key, and hybrid-failure paths discard lat/lng and parse the same captured pharmacy page.
affected_locations.label: query_agnostic_fixture_source

affected_locations.detail: The fixed rows are Seoul addresses/coordinates with captured distance values 0.14, 0.15, and 0.19 kilometres.
affected_locations.label: deterministic_fixture_evidence

affected_locations.detail: Radius filtering, sorting, and response construction prefer every non-null captured distance instead of recomputing from the caller's origin.
affected_locations.label: broken_origin_join

affected_locations.detail: The list and accessible marker text state that each captured value is metres from the current map centre; the fixture notice only warns about sample-data availability.
affected_locations.label: user_facing_sink

fix_direction: Treat fixture records as origin-independent facts and recompute distance per request, or clearly disable location assertions when the capture origin is unknown.
review_tier: P1 — misleading care-location proximity
rationale: Fixture labeling discloses sample data but does not cure the false statement that captured distance is from the caller's current map center.

source: Anonymous hospital search in fixture mode or hybrid fallback with arbitrary coordinates.
closest_control: Fixture status exists, but the query-agnostic fixture carries captured distances and the service trusts them.
sink: Fixed Seoul hospital distances drive caller-specific radius filtering, ordering, and display.
impact: Users can be shown remote hospitals as nearby or lose truly nearby options in sample/fallback operation.
preconditions: Fixture or fallback data are selected.
Caller coordinates differ from the fixture capture origin.
counterevidence: Fixture responses are labeled as sample, not live.
The fixture row count and page traversal are bounded.

evidence.claim: Fixture-only, missing-key, and hybrid failure paths ignore the caller location/radius.
Captured hospital rows contain fixed coordinates and distance values.
The service prefers captured distance for radius/order/output.

affected_locations.detail: Any anonymous caller selects valid latitude/longitude, radius, and type=hospital.
affected_locations.label: anonymous_entrypoint

affected_locations.detail: Fixture-only, missing-key, and hybrid-failure paths discard lat/lng/radius and parse the same captured hospital page.
affected_locations.label: query_agnostic_fixture_source

affected_locations.detail: The fixed rows contain Seoul addresses/coordinates and capture-origin distance values including approximately 932 and 974 metres.
affected_locations.label: deterministic_fixture_evidence

affected_locations.detail: Radius filtering, ordering, and response construction prefer every non-null captured distance rather than recomputing from the caller's origin.
affected_locations.label: broken_origin_join

affected_locations.detail: The browser asserts that the captured distance is from the current map centre while its fixture notice only cautions that availability may be stale.
affected_locations.label: user_facing_sink

fix_direction: Recompute per-caller distance from validated fixture coordinates or remove proximity claims when fixture capture origin is unavailable.
review_tier: P1 — misleading care-location proximity
rationale: The query is discarded but its response is later described as caller-relative, a deterministic origin-join error.

source: Second pharmacy caller in the same rounded cell.
closest_control: The rounded cache is the shared control.
sink: FacilityService trusts first-caller distance.
impact: No independent impact beyond the retained R01-CAN-097 cache-origin finding.
preconditions: Two exact origins map to one key before expiry.
At least one provider distance affects a boundary/order decision.
counterevidence: The cell is approximately 100 metres, limiting the error.
Six-hour expiration bounds reuse.

evidence.claim: Rounded keys alias exact origins.
Cached distances drive caller-specific decisions.

fix_direction: Track and remediate once under R01-CAN-097.
review_tier: none — exact duplicate of retained cache-origin finding
rationale: Source, control, sink, impact, preconditions, and fix are identical to R01-CAN-097.

source: Drug-detail or chat grounding request for a product/kind with more than 20 DUR rows.
closest_control: Each DUR operation requests numOfRows=20, pageNo=1 and treats that page as complete.
sink: The truncated warning list is attached to the drug card and grounding context.
impact: A clinically relevant age, pregnancy, elderly, or combination warning beyond row 20 could be silently omitted.
preconditions: The provider contract permits or actually returns more than 20 applicable rows for a requested product and warning kind.
The omitted row contains distinct safety information not represented in the first page.
counterevidence: Many product/kind combinations may have at most 20 rows.
No target fixture or observed live response establishes a totalCount above 20 for the per-product query.
proof_gaps: Obtain the official DUR pagination/totalCount contract and a real or stubbed product/kind response with more than 20 applicable rows.
Confirm whether provider ordering guarantees the most safety-critical rows are on page one.

evidence.claim: Every DUR operation is fixed to 20 rows on page one.
warningsFor treats one byKind result as complete.
The resulting list becomes the complete warnings field.

affected_locations.detail: warningsFor treats one byKind result per warning kind as complete.
affected_locations.label: entrypoint_wrapper

affected_locations.detail: Every operation is fixed to numOfRows=20 and pageNo=1 with no total-count check or subsequent page.
affected_locations.label: root_control

affected_locations.detail: The first-page result becomes the complete warnings list in the detailed drug card.
affected_locations.label: sink

fix_direction: After confirming the contract, page to completion under a bounded cap and fail closed/degrade honestly if completeness cannot be established.
review_tier: P1 — potential omission of drug safety information
rationale: The truncating implementation is proven, but material reachability depends on an unverified external cardinality/ordering fact, so central validation cannot promote it yet.

source: Caller of DurApiClient.contraindicatedWith for a product with more than 20 combination rows.
closest_control: Only page one is checked.
sink: The method would omit later partner rows.
impact: There is no shipped production call site at the immutable target.
preconditions: A future production path calls contraindicatedWith.
The product has more than 20 relevant combination rows.
counterevidence: Target-wide symbol search finds only the method and tests, not a production caller.
The method cannot affect current HTTP/chat responses.

evidence.claim: The pairwise helper intersects only the first byKind result.
Combination lookup requests only page one.

affected_locations.detail: contraindicatedWith intersects requested products only with the combination rows returned by one byKind call.
affected_locations.label: entrypoint_wrapper

affected_locations.detail: The combination operation is limited to page 1 and 20 rows.
affected_locations.label: root_control

fix_direction: If the helper becomes reachable, establish pagination completeness before use; no current security fix is required.
review_tier: none — unreachable in shipped target
rationale: The implementation flaw is latent, but production reachability is absent at target and therefore no current security impact exists.

source: Holiday early-morning evaluation of an overnight interval.
closest_control: The holiday branch omits prior-day carry logic.
sink: WeeklyHours could return closed.
impact: The flawed branch is unreachable because the production HolidayCalendar always returns false; reachable holiday misclassification is already R01-CAN-096.
preconditions: HolidayCalendar begins returning true without the weekly-hours branch being corrected.
A pharmacy's prior holiday interval crosses midnight.
counterevidence: At the immutable target, HolidayCalendar always returns false.
R01-CAN-096 already captures the reachable all-holidays-as-weekdays defect.

evidence.claim: Holiday evaluation lacks the ordinary branch's previous-day carry.
The production predicate always returns false, making the branch unreachable.

fix_direction: Include overnight holiday cases when implementing the R01-CAN-096 holiday control; do not retain a separate current finding.
review_tier: none — unreachable branch and future test debt
rationale: The code issue is real but cannot execute in the target, and the only reachable holiday impact is already represented.

source: Any drug search/detail/chat operation in default HYBRID mode when credentials are blank or an upstream request fails.
closest_control: Clients fall back to fixtures but return no origin metadata; DrugService derives source solely from global mode and maps every HYBRID result to LIVE with Instant.now.
sink: False SourceRef values drive direct cards, model context, answer dataStatus, and suppression of the fixture warning.
impact: Repository sample data are presented as freshly retrieved government facts, directly violating §2-9 provenance.
preconditions: The default HYBRID mode is active.
A blank credential or provider error triggers one or more fixture fallbacks.
counterevidence: FIXTURE-only mode is labeled fixture correctly.
A fully successful HYBRID live call has correct live origin even though the same stamping branch is used.

evidence.claim: HYBRID is the default and the government service key defaults to blank.
Blank credentials or HYBRID errors return fixtures without origin metadata.
Only global FIXTURE mode maps to fixture; HYBRID is stamped LIVE with now.
Answer dataStatus is derived from the incorrect source refs.

affected_locations.detail: The application defaults to HYBRID while an absent government key binds as an empty string.
affected_locations.label: default_configuration

affected_locations.detail: Blank credentials or a HYBRID exception returns a classpath fixture without preserving whether it was live or fixture data.
affected_locations.label: fallback_source

affected_locations.detail: SourceRef is marked FIXTURE only when the whole application mode is fixture; HYBRID fallback is unconditionally marked LIVE.
affected_locations.label: broken_control

affected_locations.detail: The false server-authored SourceRef accompanies direct drug results and grounded chat context.
affected_locations.label: response_sink

affected_locations.detail: Hybrid fallback is the default mode.
affected_locations.label: source

affected_locations.detail: An upstream error returns a fixture but does not return origin metadata.
affected_locations.label: broken_control

affected_locations.detail: Narrative fallback also loses origin metadata.
affected_locations.label: broken_control

affected_locations.detail: DUR fallback also loses origin metadata.
affected_locations.label: broken_control

affected_locations.detail: Every non-fixture-only result is stamped LIVE with Instant.now.
affected_locations.label: root_control

affected_locations.detail: The answer becomes live when all source refs say live.
affected_locations.label: sink

affected_locations.detail: The fixture warning is shown only for fixture status.
affected_locations.label: sink

affected_locations.detail: Hybrid must fall back and say so.
affected_locations.label: contract

affected_locations.detail: Only FIXTURE mode maps to fixture.
affected_locations.label: broken stamp

affected_locations.detail: Hybrid loads fixtures.
affected_locations.label: fallback examples

fix_direction: Carry per-call origin and actual retrieval time through every client/result/cache and derive response provenance from those facts; require human review under §2-9.
review_tier: P0 — explicit AGENTS §2-9 provenance invariant bypass
rationale: The target source deterministically falls back to query-independent fixtures in HYBRID mode, discards origin metadata, and then stamps every non-FIXTURE-only result LIVE with Instant.now.

source: HYBRID EasyDrug fallback.
closest_control: Origin is lost and global mode is stamped LIVE.
sink: Fixture narrative reaches cards/model context.
impact: No independent impact beyond R01-CAN-105 false provenance and R01-CAN-110 wrong narrative binding.
preconditions: EasyDrug live lookup errors in HYBRID mode.
The fixture narrative is used in the composed drug.
counterevidence: FIXTURE mode is visibly labeled.
R01-CAN-105 covers all HYBRID false-live stamping and R01-CAN-110 covers arbitrary narrative binding.

evidence.claim: HYBRID error returns a fixed fixture narrative without origin.
The narrative is merged and the result stamped LIVE in HYBRID.

affected_locations.detail: SourceRef is intended to distinguish fixture facts on every factual card.
affected_locations.label: provenance_contract

affected_locations.detail: A HYBRID exception replaces the live response with fixture efficacy, use, caution, warning, interaction, side-effect, and storage text, but Narrated carries no origin.

affected_locations.detail: The fixture narrative is merged into Drug while SourceRef is still derived only from the global mode and stamped LIVE in HYBRID.

affected_locations.detail: Fixture narrative is sent to the model as ministry wording retrieved just now from open data.

source: HYBRID name-search fallback.

source: Anonymous detail/chat request for an attacker-selected itemSeq during HYBRID fallback.
closest_control: The permission client takes the first fixture row without checking ITEM_SEQ equality.

counterevidence: FIXTURE mode is explicitly sample data, although the identity mismatch still exists.
A successful live response with a matching first row is unaffected.

Every HYBRID failure loads the same permission-detail fixture.
Requested identity/source are joined to fixture ingredients and marked live.

affected_locations.detail: The unrelated row is converted to a sourced Drug and cached under the attacker-selected search key.
affected_locations.label: sink

fix_direction: Reapply/verify normalized query-to-record binding on fallback and never cache a mismatched result; preserve explicit fixture provenance.
review_tier: P1 — wrong drug identity in care information
rationale: The query-independent fixture is deterministically presented as a name match; false-live provenance is separately retained under R01-CAN-105.

source: Anonymous ingredient search or Pass 1b lookup during HYBRID fallback.
closest_control: The fixed ibuprofen fixture bypasses the requested ingredient filter and is cached under that ingredient key.
sink: Ibuprofen rows are returned as matches for arbitrary ingredients and can enter grounding/allergy logic.
impact: Wrong medicine selection can suppress legitimate results or expose a user to irrelevant/allergy-conflicting drug information.
preconditions: The ingredient provider lookup fails in HYBRID mode.
The requested ingredient is not represented by the fixed ibuprofen fixture.
counterevidence: Invariant-6/post-processing may reject some chat answers when identity evidence conflicts.
FIXTURE mode discloses sample status, and result count is bounded.

evidence.claim: HYBRID ingredient fallback returns a fixed ibuprofen fixture without query verification.
Rows are presented/cached as ingredient matches.
The same anonymous search surface exposes ingredient lookups.

affected_locations.detail: permission_ibuprofen.json is returned without checking the requested ingredient.
affected_locations.label: root_control

affected_locations.detail: Rows are presented as ingredient matches and cached under the selected ingredient key.
affected_locations.label: sink

fix_direction: Require normalized ingredient membership before returning/caching fallback rows and propagate fixture origin; fail closed when identity cannot be established.
review_tier: P1 — wrong ingredient matching in the safety pipeline
rationale: The fallback can turn any ingredient query into ibuprofen; downstream fail-closed controls are counterevidence against universal exposure but not a correct trust boundary.

source: Drug detail/chat request during HYBRID DUR failure for an arbitrary itemSeq.
closest_control: Each warning kind loads a fixed fixture and the parser never requires returned ITEM_SEQ to equal the requested medicine.
sink: Fixed combination/age/elderly rows or an empty pregnancy fixture are attached as the requested product's complete warnings.
impact: The server can invent irrelevant contraindications or silently omit real ones for another medicine, routing around verified safety/provenance controls.
preconditions: A DUR operation fails in HYBRID for an itemSeq not represented by its fixed fixture.
The requested drug detail is still composed and returned/grounded.
counterevidence: Successful live matching responses are unaffected.
Some wrong positive warnings fail safely by over-warning, but wrong empty/omitted warnings can fail open.

evidence.claim: Each warning kind uses a fixed query-independent fixture on fallback.
Parsed warning rows are not checked against requested ITEM_SEQ.
The resulting list is attached to the requested product.

affected_locations.detail: COMBINATION uses dur_usjnt.json for every requested itemSeq on fallback.
affected_locations.label: root_control

affected_locations.detail: Parsed ITEM_SEQ values are accepted without equality to the requested itemSeq.
affected_locations.label: broken_control

affected_locations.detail: The warning list is attached to the requested product detail.
affected_locations.label: sink

affected_locations.detail: AGE uses dur_age.json for every requested itemSeq on fallback.
affected_locations.label: root_control

affected_locations.detail: PREGNANCY uses dur_empty.json for every requested itemSeq on fallback.
affected_locations.label: root_control

affected_locations.detail: ELDERLY uses dur_elderly.json for every requested itemSeq on fallback.
affected_locations.label: root_control

fix_direction: Bind every DUR row to the requested medicine, carry fallback provenance, and treat incomplete/mismatched warning retrieval as an honest blocked/unknown state. Human safety review is mandatory.
review_tier: P0 — drug-safety fail-open and §2-9 provenance bypass
rationale: The pregnancy fallback can be empty for any requested medicine and other fixed warnings can belong to another product; no identity check prevents unsafe omission or misattribution.
